title: Research Science Institute (RSI)
company: MIT / Center for Excellence in Education
description: RSI is a prestigious 6-week summer research program at MIT for high school students. Students conduct original research in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics under the mentorship of MIT professors and researchers. The program combines classroom instruction with hands-on research, culminating in a research paper and oral presentation. Research areas include mathematics, physics, biology, chemistry, computer science, and engineering.
cost: Free (fully funded — tuition, room, board, travel)
requirements: Rising seniors with exceptional STEM aptitude. Must demonstrate strong academic record and passion for research.

title: PRIMES (Program for Research in Mathematics, Engineering, and Science)
description: MIT PRIMES is a year-long research program for high school students in mathematics, computer science, and computational biology. Students work with MIT researchers on original research projects starting in February. The program has produced numerous published papers and award-winning science fair projects. Research areas span pure mathematics, theoretical computer science, and computational biology.
requirements: Strong mathematical background. Must be able to commute to MIT or participate remotely.

title: Stanford Institutes of Medicine Summer Research Program (SIMR)
description: SIMR provides an 8-week mentored research experience for high school students at Stanford's School of Medicine. Students work directly in a Stanford lab alongside graduate students and postdocs. Research areas include cancer biology, neuroscience, immunology, genetics, biochemistry, and bioengineering. The program emphasizes hands-on wet lab and computational research skills.
requirements: Rising juniors/seniors with strong interest in biomedical research. Must be 16+ years old.

title: Summer Science Research Program (SSRP)
description: SSRP at Rockefeller University immerses high school students in biomedical research for 7 weeks. Participants join active research labs at one of the world's leading biomedical research institutions. Research areas include molecular biology, neuroscience, immunology, structural biology, and bioinformatics. Students present their findings at a summer symposium.
requirements: NYC-area high school juniors interested in biomedical research.

title: Clark Scholars Program
description: The Clark Scholars Program is a 7-week intensive summer research program for high school students. Students work one-on-one with faculty mentors across a wide range of disciplines including engineering, computer science, biology, chemistry, mathematics, psychology, and humanities. Scholars receive a $750 stipend and free room and board. The program culminates in a research presentation.
requirements: High school juniors/seniors with strong academic records.

title: Summer Science Program (SSP)
description: SSP is a 39-day academic summer program where students work in teams of three to determine the orbit of a near-Earth asteroid (Astrophysics track) or research biochemistry (Biochemistry track) or study genomics (Genomics track). The astrophysics track involves telescope observations, data reduction, and orbital mechanics calculations. The biochemistry track involves protein purification, enzyme kinetics, and computational chemistry.
requirements: Rising seniors with strong aptitude in math and science.

title: Jackson Laboratory Summer Student Program
description: The Jackson Laboratory Summer Student Program is a 10-week research internship for high school and college students. Students conduct independent research projects in mammalian genetics and genomics under the guidance of JAX scientists. Research areas include cancer biology, aging, immunology, neuroscience, and computational biology. Students receive a stipend and housing.
requirements: High school juniors/seniors with interest in genetics and biomedical research. Must be 16+.

title: NIH Summer Internship Program (SIP)
description: The NIH Summer Internship Program provides high school and college students the opportunity to spend a summer working at the NIH alongside leading biomedical scientists. Research areas span all of biomedical science including neuroscience, cancer research, genomics, immunology, infectious disease, and computational biology. Students receive a stipend and work 40 hours per week for 8 weeks.
requirements: Must be 16+ and a US citizen or permanent resident.

title: Garcia Center Summer Research Program
description: The Garcia Center at Stony Brook University offers a 7-week summer research program for high school students in materials science and engineering. Students conduct original research on polymer nanocomposites, thin-film photovoltaics, and advanced materials. The program has produced numerous Intel/Regeneron Science Talent Search finalists. Students work alongside graduate students and professors.
requirements: High school students with strong interest in science and engineering.

title: COSMOS (California State Summer School for Mathematics and Science)
description: COSMOS is a 4-week residential program at UC campuses (Davis, Irvine, San Diego, Santa Cruz) for talented high school students. Students select a research cluster and work on projects in fields like robotics, marine biology, game design, biomedical devices, astrophysics, and data science. The program combines lectures with hands-on lab work and field research.
requirements: California residents in grades 8-11 with strong interest in STEM.

title: Simons Summer Research Program
description: The Simons Summer Research Program places high school students in Stony Brook University research labs for 8 weeks. Students conduct hands-on research in biology, chemistry, physics, math, computer science, and engineering under faculty mentorship. Participants receive a $4,000 stipend and present their work at a final symposium. Many alumni go on to win major science competitions.
requirements: Rising seniors with GPA 3.5+ and strong STEM interest. Must be able to commute to Stony Brook.

title: MITES Semester (MIT Introduction to Technology, Engineering, and Science)
company: MIT Office of Engineering Outreach Programs
description: MITES Semester is an online, rigorous STEM enrichment program for high school juniors that introduces students to engineering and science disciplines. Students take courses in fields like computer science, biology, and physics. The program emphasizes collaborative learning, hands-on projects, and exposure to cutting-edge research at MIT. It also serves as a pipeline to MIT's summer MITES program.
requirements: US high school juniors from underrepresented backgrounds in STEM.

title: Regeneron Science Talent Search
description: The Regeneron Science Talent Search is the nation's oldest and most prestigious pre-college science and math competition. Students submit original research projects for judging by leading scientists. The top 300 scholars receive $2,000 each, and the top 40 finalists compete for awards up to $250,000 in Washington, DC. All areas of STEM research are eligible.
requirements: High school seniors in the US or at US-run schools abroad.

title: Brookhaven National Laboratory High School Research Program
description: Brookhaven National Lab offers summer research opportunities for high school students to work alongside scientists on real research projects. Areas include particle physics, nuclear physics, materials science, environmental science, computational science, and biology. Students gain hands-on experience with advanced equipment including the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC) and the National Synchrotron Light Source.
requirements: Must be 16+ and a US citizen or permanent resident. Must be able to commute to BNL.

title: MIT Lincoln Laboratory Beaver Works Summer Institute (BWSI)
description: BWSI offers a 4-week summer program for rising high school seniors in STEM. Courses include autonomous vehicles (RACECAR), cognitive assistants, embedded security, medlytics (medical analytics), and drone autonomy. Students build real systems and compete in final challenges. The program is project-based with intensive hands-on engineering and programming.
requirements: Rising seniors with programming experience. Prerequisite online course required.

title: Research in Science and Engineering (RISE)
description: BU RISE is a 6-week summer research internship for rising high school seniors. Students join research labs across BU's College of Engineering, College of Arts and Sciences, and School of Medicine. Research areas include neuroscience, nanotechnology, materials science, computer science, and biomedical engineering. Students receive mentorship and present at a final symposium.
requirements: Rising seniors with strong STEM background. Must be 15+.

title: Stanford Humanities Institute Summer Research Program
description: Stanford's Humanities Institute offers a summer program for high school students interested in humanities research. Students explore topics in history, philosophy, literature, political science, and cultural studies through seminars and independent research projects. The program emphasizes critical analysis, argumentation, and academic writing.
requirements: High school sophomores and juniors with strong academic records.

title: NASA OSSI (One Stop Shopping Initiative) Internships
description: NASA offers summer internships at its centers across the US for high school students. Projects span aerospace engineering, computer science, planetary science, astrophysics, biology, and materials science. Students work on real NASA missions and research projects under the mentorship of NASA scientists and engineers.
requirements: US citizens, 16+, GPA 3.0+. Must pass background check.

title: Broad Summer Scholars Program
company: Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
description: The Broad Summer Scholars Program places high school students in genomics and computational biology research labs. Students work on projects in areas like cancer genomics, infectious disease, single-cell biology, and machine learning for biology. The program provides hands-on experience with cutting-edge genomics technologies and computational tools.
requirements: High school juniors/seniors. Strong interest in biology and/or computation.

title: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory DNA Learning Center
description: CSHL offers research programs for high school students in molecular biology, genetics, and neuroscience. Students participate in hands-on experiments using professional lab equipment and learn techniques like DNA extraction, PCR, gel electrophoresis, and bioinformatics. The program provides exposure to world-class research in a renowned genetics laboratory setting.
requirements: High school students interested in molecular biology and genetics.

title: Polygence Research Mentorship
description: Polygence connects high school students with expert mentors (PhD students, postdocs, professors) for 1-on-1 research projects in any field. Students produce a research paper, podcast, app, or other tangible output. Areas span all disciplines: AI/ML, neuroscience, economics, literature, public health, environmental science, and more. Flexible scheduling with sessions 2-3 times per month.
requirements: High school students with a specific research interest. No prior research experience required.

title: Lumiere Research Scholar Program
description: Lumiere pairs high school students with PhD and postdoctoral researchers from Harvard, MIT, Stanford, and other top universities for 1-on-1 mentored research projects. Students produce a research paper suitable for publication. Areas include AI, economics, neuroscience, environmental science, public policy, computer science, and biology. The program runs year-round with flexible scheduling.
requirements: High school students. Application includes a short essay on research interests.

title: LaunchX Entrepreneurship Summer Program
description: LaunchX is a summer program where students launch real startups. While not traditional lab research, students conduct market research, customer discovery, product development, and data-driven decision making. Students work in teams to build and launch actual companies. The program emphasizes innovation, problem-solving, and evidence-based entrepreneurship.
requirements: High school students with entrepreneurial interest.

title: Caltech WAVE Fellows Program
description: The WAVE Fellows program at Caltech provides summer research opportunities for students in STEM. While primarily for undergraduates, exceptional high school seniors are sometimes considered. Students work with Caltech faculty on cutting-edge research in physics, chemistry, biology, computer science, and engineering. Research areas include astrophysics, quantum computing, and climate science.
requirements: Strong STEM background. Must be from underrepresented backgrounds in STEM.

title: Aspiring Scientists Summer Internship Program (ASSIP)
description: ASSIP matches high school students with George Mason University professors for a 7-week research experience. Students conduct original research projects in fields such as computational neuroscience, bioinformatics, cybersecurity, environmental science, and data analytics. Many alumni have presented at national conferences and published in peer-reviewed journals.
requirements: High school students. Strong interest in STEM research.
